title: DTD Editor
text: DTD Editor The DTD Editor allows you to load, create, or edit Document Type Definitions (DTDs) for XML data, and map them to Uniface entities and fields. The DTD Editor enables you to: Create a Uniface DTD based on modeled entities. Create a Uniface DTD in the Repository from a DTD in your file system. Create a Uniface DTD from the data structure of a Uniface component. Validate the DTD to ensure it is syntactically correct. Map XML elements to Uniface fields and entities, so that Uniface can transport data to and from the XML stream. Define additional properties for the DTD. For example, you can define a URI location (an Internet address) for the DTD so that non-Uniface components can locate the DTD. Figure 1. Define DTD Related Links Uniface-Compliant DTDs A Document Type Definition (DTD) defines rules for the structure of an XML stream. In Uniface, a DTD is a development object that includes DTD definitions and a default mapping between the XML elements defined by the DTD and Uniface component fields and entities. It can be used to define the rules for the structure of an XML stream that is exchanged between components. Working with DTDs Manually Defining DTDs Use the DTD Editor to define DTDs. Uniface uses DTDs to read and write XML streams. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
